subject: [PATCH v4 07/10] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools (implemented as buffer ring)

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch introduces a buffer pool feature to the fuse io-uring implementation, allowing the server to pass in a contiguous allocation of buffers that can be used on demand by the kernel for servicing requests. This decouples the number of buffers required from the number of entries in the ring, reducing memory usage and enabling future optimizations such as incremental buffer consumption. The patch adds support for a FUSE_URING_BUFPOOL flag during registration and introduces a buffer ring data structure to manage buffer selection and recycling. The server must provide the payload buffer pool through an iovec payload in the first sqe, and the kernel will select and recycle buffers from this pool as needed.

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the fuse io-uring interface is inconsistent because it decouples buffers from sqes but not registration, and requested that registration be decoupled as well.
reviews.raw_body:
I find this interface inconsistent.   Decoupling the buffer(s) from
the seq's means that the registration should be decoupled as well, no?

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: reviewer questioned the mapping of buf_id to buffer address, suggesting it would be simpler to provide the buffer address directly
reviews.raw_body:
How does buf_id map to a buffer address?

Wouldn't it be simpler to provide the buffer address directly?

We also need some uAPI documentation (generally not just for this
patchset).  What about a new directory:
Documentation/filesystems/fuse/uapi?

Thanks,
Milklos

2026-06-15.patch_summary: This patch adds support for buffer pools to the fuse io-uring implementation, allowing the server to pass in a contiguous allocation of buffers that can be used as needed by the kernel. This decouples the number of entries from the number of buffers required, reducing memory usage and enabling future optimizations such as incremental buffer consumption. The server must set the FUSE_URING_BUFPOOL flag during registration and provide the payload buffer pool through the iovec payload in the first sqe. A buffer ring data structure is used to manage the buffer pool, selecting and recycling buffers on demand.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about queue setup and configuration, proposing to add a new command operation (FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_QUEUE_SETUP) to pass in queue-level settings, including the number of reduced queues, buffer pool, and flags. This would decouple queue creation from sqe registration and provide a clean way to set up queue configurations.
reviews.raw_body:
Looking at Bernd's reduced queue series [1], I think we'd most likely
want to also pass in the number of reduced queues separate from sqe
registration. I think along the lines of the config sqe Bernd brought
up, it seems cleanest to add a new cmd op
(FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_QUEUE_SETUP) where queue-level settings (eg flags,
number of queues, bufpool, etc) gets passed in. This would have the
nice side effect of doing the queue creation in this op instead of
right now creating the queue lazily when the first sqe gets
registered. On the userspace side, this adds a bit more friction (eg a
two-phase setup where userspace submits the queue setup cmd, reaps its
completion, and reuses that sqe to register an entry), but that seems
like a reasonable tradeoff.

Does this sound ok to you and Bernd?

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/fuse-devel/20260529-reduced-nr-ring-queues_3-v5-0-1dc08c2fccf6@bsbernd.com/T/#u

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
The author addressed Miklos Szeredi's concern about how the server would access specific buffers within the buffer pool, explaining that the server knows the starting address and size of each buffer and can compute the address from the buf_id.

The author addressed Miklos Szeredi's concern about how to return the selected buffer in fuse_uring_ent_in_out, proposing to use a buf_id instead of the buffer address and discussing potential future implications.

Author agrees with Miklos Szeredi's suggestion, confirms they will add uAPI documentation and update the directory.
reviews.raw_body:
The server knows the starting address of the bufpool (since it
allocated it) and it knows the size of each buffer in the pool. It can
compute from the buf_id the address corresponding to the buffer.

---

This would have to be returned as part of the struct
fuse_uring_ent_in_out. There's currently 12 bytes that are free to
use. Returning buf_id takes up 2 bytes and I think is better than
using 8 bytes to provide the buffer address. However, returning the
buf id makes adding incremental buffer consumption in the future a bit
trickier (eg the server would need to keep track of the offset into
the previously used buffer and do any alignment rounding up). Maybe a
good compromise is returning a uint32_t offset that corresponds to the
offset in the bufpool? The buf size is a uint32_t, so I think this
would work just as well as providing the buffer address directly.

---

That sounds good. I'll write some uAPI documentation for this and add
it to that new directory.

Thanks,
Joanne

This decouples the number of entries from the number of buffers required, reducing memory usage and enabling future optimizations such as incremental buffer consumption. The server must set the FUSE_URING_BUFPOOL flag during registration and provide the payload buffer pool through the iovec payload in the first sqe. A buffer ring data structure is used to manage the buffer pool, selecting and recycling buffers on demand.
